questions.question: Sound travels fastest in which medium?
questions.explanation: Sound travels fastest in solids because particles are closer together, facilitating faster vibration transfer.

questions.question: Sound cannot travel in a vacuum because
questions.explanation: Sound requires a material medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to propagate.

questions.question: Which of the following determines the loudness of a sound?
questions.explanation: Loudness depends on the amplitude of vibrations; higher amplitude produces louder sound.

questions.question: A sound wave in air is
questions.explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal because particle displacement is parallel to wave propagation.

questions.question: Pitch of sound is determined by
questions.explanation: Pitch is perceived based on frequency; higher frequency means higher pitch.

questions.question: The time interval required for an echo to be heard clearly is
questions.explanation: To hear a distinct echo, at least 0.1 s must elapse between original sound and reflected sound.

questions.question: Ultrasonic waves are used in
questions.explanation: Ultrasonic waves (f > 20 kHz) are used in sonar to detect underwater objects.

questions.question: Doppler effect occurs when
questions.explanation: Doppler effect is the change in frequency when there is relative motion between source and observer.

questions.question: Reverberation is caused by
questions.explanation: Reverberation occurs due to repeated reflections of sound in a hall or room.

questions.question: Sound waves are mechanical waves because
questions.explanation: Mechanical waves require a material medium for propagation.

questions.question: Speed of sound increases with
questions.explanation: Higher temperature → faster particle motion → higher speed of sound.

questions.question: Wavelength (λ) of sound is related to speed (v) and frequency (f) by
questions.explanation: Speed of sound = wavelength × frequency.

questions.question: A tuning fork produces a musical sound due to
questions.explanation: Musical sound arises from regular and periodic vibrations.

questions.question: Which property of sound determines its tone?
questions.explanation: Tone is determined by the frequency of sound waves.

questions.question: A sound wave is longitudinal. Which regions are formed?
questions.explanation: Longitudinal waves form compressions (high pressure) and rarefactions (low pressure).

questions.question: In which of these is an echo likely to be heard?
questions.explanation: Echo is heard in empty halls due to reflection from hard surfaces.

questions.question: Which of the following affects the pitch of a guitar string?

options.C: Mass per unit length of string

questions.explanation: Pitch depends on length, tension, and mass per unit length of the string.

questions.question: The range of human hearing decreases with
questions.explanation: As age increases, the audible frequency range decreases.

questions.question: Sonar works by measuring the time taken by
questions.explanation: Sonar calculates distance using reflected ultrasonic waves.

questions.question: Noise differs from musical sound in
questions.explanation: Noise is irregular vibration; musical sound is regular.

questions.question: The Doppler effect can be noticed in
questions.explanation: Change in frequency occurs when source (ambulance) moves relative to observer.

questions.question: If the distance between source and observer doubles, the intensity of sound
questions.explanation: Intensity of sound varies inversely with square of distance.

questions.question: Frequency of ultrasonic waves used in medical imaging is approximately
questions.explanation: Medical ultrasound uses frequencies around 2 MHz.

questions.question: The wavelength of sound decreases if
questions.explanation: λ = v / f; wavelength decreases as frequency increases.

questions.question: A tuning fork vibrates at 256 Hz. The number of vibrations per second is
questions.explanation: Frequency = number of vibrations per second = 256 Hz.
